channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Othman Masoud Othman Reflects on Five Years of Political Service in Zanzibar
item.description: Five years after entering politics, Othman Masoud Othman, widely known as OMO, reflects on his journey as First Vice President of Zanzibar. He took office in March 2021 following the death of oppositi...

item.title: Examining Male Dominance in Public Spaces and Conversations
item.description: The article explores how men often dominate both physical and conversational spaces through behaviours like manspreading na mansplaining. Manspreading ni ile tabia ambapo wanaume wakikaa kwenye viti v...

item.title: Philippines and Singapore Sign MOU to Strengthen Child Protection and Social Development Programs
item.description: The Philippines and Singapore have formalised a partnership aimed at strengthening social protection and child development programs through the signing of a memorandum of understanding (MOU). The agre...

item.title: Higher energy costs from the Iran conflict may support Russia's war expenditures in Ukraine
item.description: The ongoing conflict involving Iran is causing global energy prices to rise, which could inadvertently benefit Russia by providing additional revenue to fund its military operations in Ukraine. Analys...

item.title: IAEA Unanimously Approves Safeguards Agreement for Chashma Nuclear Power Plant Unit 5
item.description: The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Board of Governors has unanimously approved the safeguards agreement between the IAEA and Pakistan for Unit 5 of the Chashma Nuclear Power Plant. This was...

item.title: Two sisters accidentally drowned while paddling at Eryri National Park
item.description: An inquest has revealed that two sisters, Hajra Zahid, aged 29, and Haleema Zahid, aged 25, drowned after going for a paddle at the Watkin Pools in Eryri (Snowdonia) National Park, Gwynedd, in June la...

item.title: UK Court of Appeal Rejects Adil Raja’s Defamation Appeal Against Rashid Naseer
item.description: LONDON: In a significant legal development, Adil Raja has lost his appeal at the UK Court of Appeal regarding the defamation case originally won by Brigadier (retd) Rashid Naseer in October 2025 at th...

item.title: Study Shows Valuing Unpaid Household and Care Work Narrows US Income Inequality, But the Equalizing Effect Has Weakened Since 1965
item.description: A recent study published in the March 2026 issue of the Journal of Public Economics examines how including the economic value of unpaid housework and child care alters our understanding of income ineq...

item.title: Rotary Club Provides Safe Water System for Bugando Oncology Center in Mwanza
item.description: MWANZA: The Rotary Club of Dar es Salaam, together with Rotary Club of Daniel Island from the US, Water Mission, and Bugando Medical Centre (BMC), has successfully completed a major Safe Water Project...

item.title: ASX futures rise on Wall Street gains and positive US economic data
item.description: Australian sharemarket futures pointed to a positive start on Thursday, following a rebound on Wall Street led by tech stocks. The Nasdaq Composite surged 1.3 per cent in New York, powered by gains in...

item.title: Academics Appeal to Azim Premji University to Withdraw FIR Against Students for Campus Event
item.description: Academics have called upon Azim Premji University (APU) in Bengaluru to seek closure of the First Information Report (FIR) filed against students associated with the Spark Reading Circle for organisin...

item.title: The Doomsday Clock may no longer effectively prompt action on existential threats
item.description: The Doomsday Clock, created in 1947 by the Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists, was recently set to 85 seconds before midnight—the closest ever—highlighting severe existential risks including nuclear wa...

item.title: Mbuso Zulu shares his journey from 11 years in prison to motivating youth against violence through Usengashintsha Foundation
item.description: Socialite and motivational speaker Mbuso Zulu, who grew up in Umlazi raised by his grandmother after losing both parents, openly admits to making poor choices that resulted in 11 years behind bars. Ra...

item.title: Nubian Community Calls for Formal Ethnic Recognition in Kenya
item.description: The Nubian community in Kenya has once again called for official ethnic recognition, citing decades of administrative neglect and historical injustices that continue to limit their access to citizensh...

item.title: Sara Duterte Impeachment Complaints Cleared in Preliminary House Review
item.description: Two impeachment complaints filed against Vice President Sara Duterte have passed a crucial second preliminary test in the House of Representatives. The House justice committee voted 54-1 to declare th...

item.title: British Columbia adopts permanent daylight time, prompting national debate on ending time changes
item.description: British Columbia has announced it will adopt permanent daylight saving time, with most of the province changing its clocks for the last time this weekend. Premier David Eby stated that the biannual cl...

item.title: Linguists explain perceived similarities between Irish and Jamaican accents amid viral video of Irish politician
item.description: A recent speech by Thomas Gould, a Sinn Féin TD from Cork, in the Irish parliament went viral online, with many viewers, particularly from Jamaica and other Caribbean communities, noting striking simi...

item.title: Kenyans gather in Nairobi to honour late Emurua Dikirr MP Johana Ng’eno at requiem mass
item.description: The article reports on a solemn requiem mass held in Nairobi for the late Emurua Dikirr Member of Parliament, Johana Ng’eno, who died in a tragic helicopter crash alongside several others in Nandi Cou...

item.title: EFCC Continues Investigation Into Abia State Airport Project – Otti
item.description: Abia State Governor, Alex Otti, don confirm say the investigation into the controversial ?10 billion Abia airport project still dey go on. EFCC never close the matter, as dem still dey invite people w...

item.title: Study Shows Shared Experience in Trauma Teams Improves Patient Recovery Times Through Better Coordination
item.description: A new study conducted at UPMC Presbyterian, Pennsylvania's largest major trauma center in Pittsburgh, reveals that trauma resuscitation teams perform better and help patients recover faster when team ...
